task: Unified all spoken announcements in the voice subsystem through a single `announce()` pipeline, changed the default voice from Samantha to Zarvox, and fixed empty-text handling in the `speak()` function to prevent spurious subprocess calls.
discussion_summary: The voice subsystem had multiple entry points for spoken notifications, each using different voice configurations. Prior sessions refactored all call sites to route through a single `announce()` function and changed the default voice to Zarvox per user preference. This session discovered and fixed a bug: `speak_signature()` correctly skipped empty prefix/body strings (per a settled decision), but `speak()` — the path `announce()` uses in single-voice mode — did not guard against empty messages and would call `say ""` anyway. The fix adds an early return in `speak()` when text is empty, and comprehensive tests verify the behavior across all voice modes.

accomplishments.what: Refactored all spoken announcements to use unified `announce()` pipeline
Verified all bare `speak()` call sites eliminated and routed through `announce()`
Changed default single-voice mode from Samantha to Zarvox
Confirmed test suite passes after voice refactor
Committed and pushed voice unification (00bd902) and default voice change (80d2625)
Fixed empty-text handling in `speak()` to skip subprocess call when message is empty
Added comprehensive tests for empty-string handling in `speak()`, `speak_signature()`, and `announce()`
Verified full test suite (173 tests) passes after empty-text fix

next_actions.action: Test announce() pipeline with all voice types (single-voice Zarvox, dual-voice Good News + Zarvox, signature mode) to verify Zarvox default is applied correctly across all modes
next_actions.why: Open goal from this session; ensures the Zarvox default works consistently across all voice configurations

next_actions.action: Document voice subsystem API changes and migration guide for Zarvox default in README or VOICE_API.md
next_actions.why: Open goal from this session; helps future developers understand the unified announce() pipeline and voice configuration

decisions.decision: Default single-voice mode uses Zarvox instead of Samantha
decisions.reason: User preference: Samantha sounds too similar to Siri; Zarvox is the preferred default

decisions.decision: All spoken announcements route through unified `announce()` pipeline
decisions.reason: Eliminates inconsistent voice configuration across quota warnings, permission prompts, session-done pings, and other notifications

decisions.decision: Empty prefix or body strings are valid signals to skip that part of the announcement
decisions.reason: Allows callers to conditionally suppress voice output without special-casing logic; `speak()` and `speak_signature()` both guard against empty text

decisions.decision: `speak()` function skips subprocess call when text is empty
decisions.reason: Prevents spurious `say ""` calls that waste system resources; aligns with the settled decision that empty strings are valid skip signals

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Keep Samantha as the default voice for single-voice mode
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: defaulting to samantha will make it sound like siri, let's default to something else no G, for me zarvox works find as default as well

relational_files.why: Core voice pipeline; imported by stop.py, notification.py, and other hook modules; fixed empty-text handling in speak() this session
Uses announce() for session-done ping; refactored in prior session
Uses announce() for quota warnings and permission-prompt alerts; refactored in prior session
Defines default voice (VOICE_DEFAULT); changed from Samantha to Zarvox in prior session
Tests voice pipeline; added comprehensive empty-text tests this session; all 173 tests passing
